description: Find ProgramDescriptor examples
Find reader kernel examples

Existing code is a staged TDD draft handling only pinned shape (1,1,128,128). Need to add: multi-block Q/KV loops, multi-core distribution, GQA/MQA head replication, mask handling, proper O accumulation and l_i update, output normalization. Key issues: Stage 11 add is missing, Stage 12 O+=PV add is missing, uses cb_sum_new instead of cb_sum_old for normalization, single-block only, single-core only.

error: Hang at Stage 12 PV matmul (compute_kernel.cpp:546). TRISC0 stuck at matmul_block in1_buf.wait_front(cb_v), TRISC2 stuck at reserve_back(cb_o_accum). Writer stuck at cb_wait_front(cb_out). Reader stuck at wait_for_brisc_notification. Root cause: cb_v has 2*D_t pages (8) but PV matmul needs B_kv_t*D_t=16 tiles in one K-block. cb_o_accum has B_q_t*D_t=16 pages but matmul tries to reserve row_group_tiles for TileRowMajor.

description: Rewrite all kernels for full Flash Attention. Key design decisions: (1) Use SubblockMajor tile_order for matmul (simpler CB layout, avoids TileRowMajor row_group reserve issues). (2) PV matmul with packer_l1_acc=false, num_k_blocks=1 - output to cb_o_accum, then eltwise add into cb_o. (3) For the O accumulation, use a separate cb_o_accum for PV matmul output, then add to cb_o in-place. (4) Properly handle scaler CB: reader pushes 2 scaler tiles, compute pops after each reduce. (5) Handle multi-block Q/KV loops, multi-core, GQA, mask.

error: Hang at compute_kernel.cpp:220 (add<cb_sum_old, cb_sum_new, cb_sum_old>). TRISC0 stuck in BinaryFpu wait_per_tile on cb_sum_old(30) or cb_sum_new(29). Phase 11 add: cb_sum_old was used in-place in phase 7 mul, then phase 11 tries to read it again with Streaming. The in-place mul in phase 7 consumed cb_sum_old tiles (Streaming pop), but the in-place add in phase 11 needs to wait for new tiles to be pushed. The issue: phase 7 pops cb_sum_old (in-place mul), leaving it empty. Phase 11 add tries to read cb_sum_old which is empty.

description: CB lifecycle issue: Phase 7 mul<cb_sum_old, cb_alpha, cb_sum_old> is in-place Streaming — it pops tiles and pushes them back. But the in-place chain pops BEFORE pushing, so after phase 7, cb_sum_old has B_q_t tiles at the front again (the rescaled values). Phase 11 add<cb_sum_old, cb_sum_new, cb_sum_old> should work: it reads from both, pops both, pushes to cb_sum_old. BUT: the chain might not handle in-place correctly when the same CB is both input A and output. Need to verify the chain handles pop-before-reserve correctly. Actually, the real issue may be that phase 7 used HeldBulk for cb_alpha but then phase 11 uses Streaming for cb_alpha. Phase 7: mul<cb_sum_old, cb_alpha, cb_sum_old, None, Streaming, Streaming>. This pops both cb_sum_old and cb_alpha. So cb_alpha is empty after phase 7. Phase 11: add<cb_sum_old, cb_sum_new, cb_sum_old>. This reads cb_sum_old (which has tiles from phase 7 rescale) and cb_sum_new (which has tiles from phase 10 reduce). Both should have tiles. The hang is TRISC0 waiting on wait_per_tile for BinaryFpu with CBs 30 and 29. Let me check if cb_sum_new actually has tiles pushed by the reduce.

The hang is at Phase 11 add. Let me check if cb_sum_new actually has tiles. The reduce<SUM, WaitUpfrontNoPop> should push B_q_t tiles. But maybe the issue is that the SUM reduce uses the matmul path (SUM REDUCE_ROW uses matmul), and the matmul state was left dirty by Phase 9 unary<Exp>. The reduce helper with reconfig_mode=INPUT_AND_OUTPUT (default) should reconfig. But maybe the matmul init for the reduce is not happening correctly after the eltwise chain. Let me also check: after Phase 4 reduce<MAX>, I pop the MAX scaler. Then Phase 5 eltwise_chain (alpha) runs. Then Phases 6-9 eltwise. Then Phase 10 reduce<SUM>. The reduce<SUM> needs to wait for cb_scaler_reduce. After popping MAX scaler, the SUM scaler should be at front. But Phase 10 reduce does scaler_dfb.wait_front(1) — it waits for 1 tile. The SUM scaler should be there. Let me try removing the scaler pop after Phase 4 and see if that helps — maybe the pop is causing an issue.

error: static_assert in BinaryFpu: (BIndex, BPolicy) illegal for Block. Root cause: Phase 8 sub uses OperandKind::Col with InputLifecycle::HeldStream, but Col requires non-draining lifecycles (Bulk/HeldBulk). Fix: use HeldBulk instead of HeldStream for cb_max_new in Phase 8.

description: Read helper headers
Analyze CB sync issues
findings: F1: Phase 11 outputs to cb_sum_new instead of cb_sum_old (revert to in-place). F2: Scaler tiles prepared once but consumed per KV-block (need re-push). F3: Writer total_tiles includes num_q_blocks but compute only outputs on last Q-block. Also: B_q_t/B_kv_t are fixed at 4 but test shapes like (1,1,32,32) have only 1 tile — reader reads out-of-bounds tiles.

description: Check packer_l1_acc semantics

Root cause for basic test hang: B_Q_T=4 and B_KV_T=4 are fixed, but test shape (1,1,32,32) has only 1 tile in each dim. Reader reads out-of-bounds tiles. Fix: make B_Q_T and B_KV_T dynamic — min(4, S_q_tiles) and min(4, S_kv_tiles). Pass actual block sizes as runtime args. Also fix: (1) Phase 11 revert to in-place on cb_sum_old. (2) Reader re-push scalers per KV-block. (3) Writer total_tiles = num_work_units * num_o_tiles (not * num_q_blocks). (4) Compute writes output every Q-block.
Read helper header signatures
Find split_work_to_cores usage
Check reduce helper scaler handling
Check reduce scaler pop and fill helpers

Full rewrite of all 3 kernels + program descriptor. Key design decisions: (1) Reader streams Q-block then K/V/mask blocks per KV iteration, handles GQA head mapping. (2) Compute: 13-phase flash attention recurrence with proper CB lifecycle for each phase. Key issues to fix: scaler CB needs separate handling for MAX (row-0 fill) vs SUM (col-0 fill) reduce — reader prepares fresh scaler before each reduce. Phase 5 eltwise_chain for alpha must use HeldBulk for both m_old and m_new since Col broadcast needs non-draining lifecycle. Phase 6 mul O*=alpha uses Col broadcast — alpha needs HeldBulk. Phase 8 sub S-=m_new uses Col broadcast — m_new needs HeldBulk. Phase 11 add l_i+=l_blk: output to cb_sum_old in-place is problematic if cb_sum_old was Streaming-popped in phase 7. Solution: phase 7 mul outputs to cb_sum_old in-place Streaming (pop+push), phase 11 reads from cb_sum_old Streaming (pop) and cb_sum_new Streaming (pop), outputs to cb_sum_old (push). Phase 12 PV matmul outputs to cb_o_accum, then add cb_o += cb_o_accum. Writer drains cb_out after normalization.

error: static_assert: OperandKind::Block is illegal with InputLifecycle::Streaming in mul<cb_o, cb_sum_old, cb_out, Col>

description: OperandKind::Block cannot be used with InputLifecycle::Streaming. Change to OperandKind::Scalar for the first operand in mul calls with Col broadcast.

error: 1) reconfig template param in wrong position in matmul_block; 2) Recip not declared - missing eltwise_math.hpp include

description: Complete rewrite of all kernels + program descriptor. Key issues to fix from prior WIP: (1) Block sizes B_Q_T/B_KV_T are compile-time fixed at 4 — must handle shapes with fewer tiles by making block sizes dynamic via min(4, S_tiles). (2) OperandKind::Block cannot be used with InputLifecycle::Streaming — use Scalar or Col/Row correctly. (3) Scaler tiles need re-push per KV-block (reduce pops them). (4) Phase 11 add must not be in-place on cb_sum_old if it was already popped. (5) compute_kernel_hw_startup needs SrcOrder::Reverse for matmul. (6) Writer total_tiles must match compute output count. (7) m_i/l_i/O_i init must be per-Q-block, not per-kernel.

error: TRISC0 stuck in BinaryFpu wait_per_tile on cb_sum_old(30) or cb_sum_new(29) at line 201 (Phase 11: add<cb_sum_old, cb_sum_new, cb_sum_old>). Writer stuck at cb_wait_front(cb_out). Reader also stuck. The compute kernel is at Phase 11 add — same hang location as the drafter hit.

description: Phase 11 add<cb_sum_old, cb_sum_new, cb_sum_old>(B_q_t) uses default OperandKind::Scalar for both A and B. But cb_sum_old and cb_sum_new are Col-shaped (B_q_t x 1 tiles). OperandKind::Scalar means the chain iterates as 1x1 — only processing 1 tile, then waiting for more from each CB. Since both CBs have exactly B_q_t tiles, and the chain expects 1 tile from each per iteration but loops B_q_t times, this might actually work. BUT: the real issue may be that in-place Streaming on cb_sum_old causes a CB deadlock: the chain pops cb_sum_old tile, then tries to reserve_back on cb_sum_old to push the result, but the CB might be full. Actually, with Streaming input (PerTile wait, PerTile pop) and Streaming output (PerTile reserve, PerTile push), the in-place pattern should work: pop 1, push 1, pop 1, push 1... The CB has B_q_t pages. After phase 7, cb_sum_old has B_q_t tiles. But wait — does the reader push new tiles for cb_sum_old? The reader pushes B_q_t tiles at Q-block init, then phase 7 rescales them in-place. After phase 7, cb_sum_old has B_q_t tiles. Phase 11 add reads cb_sum_old (Streaming pop) + cb_sum_new (Streaming pop), outputs to cb_sum_old (push). This should work with B_q_t pages. Unless the issue is that cb_sum_new does NOT have B_q_t tiles — maybe the SUM reduce with WaitUpfrontNoPop pushed 0 tiles?

error: device timeout, potential hang - cores 24-17, 20-16, 23-16 stuck

symptom: BRISC stuck at cb_wait_front in writer kernel line 24 — compute never pushed to cb_out

description: Study matmul_block helper API

Compile error: constexpr division by zero on CT args. Need to cast to runtime or use if-guarded non-constexpr. Also need to fix matmul shape params (of() takes 7 args: in0_num_subblocks, in1_num_subblocks, out_subblock_h, out_subblock_w, in0_block_k, num_k_blocks, batch). Also need to fix CB lifecycle issues across Q-block and KV-block loops.

Hang at phase 11 add<cb_sum_old, cb_sum_new, cb_sum_old>. TRISC0 waiting on cb_sum_new (CB 29). Need to check if SUM reduce with WaitUpfrontNoPop actually pushes output tiles correctly, and if CB sizes are sufficient.

error: PCC -0.047760 < threshold 0.995 — output is essentially uncorrelated with reference. Kernel runs without hang but produces wrong values.

description: PCC ~0 means output is random/uncorrelated. Likely causes: (1) Matmul transpose direction wrong — Q@K^T might produce wrong tile ordering. (2) V tile ordering wrong for PV matmul. (3) Flash attention recurrence logic wrong (e.g. m_i/l_i not updating correctly). (4) Output tile ordering wrong in writer. Need to use deterministic debug test + DEVICE_PRINT to isolate which phase diverges.
Check reduce scaler handling
Check eltwise in-place ordering

Drafter compute kernel has multiple bugs: (1) matmul_block Shape.of args wrong order, (2) Q retention across KV blocks not handled, (3) m_old/l_old persistence across KV blocks wrong, (4) scaler CB pop after reduce is wrong (reduce never pops scaler), (5) in-place on cb_sum_old/sum_new backwards, (6) O accumulation strategy wrong (packer_l1_acc=false but should accumulate differently), (7) missing outer loop over work units, (8) missing output normalization by l_i

error: Timeout after clearing build cache. Compile succeeded but kernel hangs at runtime. No triage report generated.

description: Hang after compile succeeds. Possible causes: (1) Reader/Writer/Compute CB sync mismatch — one pushes, another waits. (2) Reader init state push per Q-block but compute also needs to wait for it. (3) Reader RT args layout mismatch with kernel expectations. (4) Compute reads num_work_units from RT arg[0] but descriptor sends [units_this_core, S_q_tiles, S_kv_tiles, ...] — the compute kernel only expects [num_work_units] as RT arg, which is correct. Need to run with --dev and check triage.
Static analysis of SDPA kernels

Static analyzer found 5 issues: (F1) Phase 6 mul O*=alpha uses InputLifecycle::Bulk for in-place cb_o — BulkReservePerTile output tries reserve_back(16) on full CB. Fix: use Streaming. (F2) Phase 8 sub S-=m_new uses Bulk for in-place cb_scores_masked — same deadlock. Fix: use Streaming. (F3) Phase 10 SUM reduce uses WaitAndPopPerTile — consumes cb_exp_scores before PV matmul. Fix: use WaitUpfrontNoPop. (F4) cb_max_old never popped at Q-block end — reader deadlocks on second Q-block. Fix: pop cb_max_old. (F5) Phase 14 mul uses Bulk on cb_o — secondary, only if earlier phases fixed. Also note: the linter changed the compute kernel significantly — removed comments, changed mm_block_init params, changed matmul shapes, removed Phase 0 waits. Need to restore correct structure.

Linter already applied static analyzer fixes (Streaming for in-place, WaitUpfrontNoPop for SUM reduce, cb_max_old pop at Q-block end). Linter also changed: TileRowMajor instead of SubblockMajor, removed Phase 0 waits, changed mm_block_init params, added cb_wait_front before cb_pop_front for scalers. Let me test the current linter-modified version.

error: Still hanging at Phase 11 add<cb_sum_old, cb_sum_new, cb_sum_old> line 230. TRISC0 stuck in BinaryFpu wait_per_tile on CBs 30+29. TRISC1 stuck in matmul_configure_addrmod (reduce matmul init). TRISC2 stuck in packer sync. The issue might be: (1) in-place add on cb_sum_old deadlocks, or (2) reduce matmul init conflicts with prior matmul state. Try: output Phase 11 add to cb_sum_new instead of in-place, then swap roles for next phase.

description: The in-place add<cb_sum_old, cb_sum_new, cb_sum_old> deadlocks because: with OperandKind::Scalar + Streaming, per tile: wait A(1), wait B(1), pop A(1), pop B(1), reserve out(1)=A(1), push out(1)=A(1). But the eltwise_chain processes BinaryFpu then PackTile. The BinaryFpu wait_per_tile waits for BOTH A and B tiles before computing. Then PackTile reserves+pushes output. The issue is: the BinaryFpu pops A and B AFTER computing (at the end of the chain element), not before the PackTile. So the sequence is: wait A, wait B, compute, [PackTile: reserve A, pack, push A, pop A, pop B]. The reserve_back on A happens BEFORE pop_front on A — if the CB is full (all B_q_t pages occupied), reserve_back blocks. Fix: output to a DIFFERENT CB (cb_sum_new), then use cb_sum_new as the running sum for the next iteration.

details: BRISC (writer) stuck at cb_wait_front on cb_out. TRISC0 (compute) stuck at BinaryFpu wait_per_tile on cb_sum_old (index 30) in eltwise_chain add<30,29,30> at compute.cpp:230 (phase 11: l_i += l_blk). TRISC1/2 in matmul init state. The compute is stuck waiting for cb_sum_old in phase 11 add, which means cb_sum_old is empty. Phase 7 (mul<cb_sum_old, cb_alpha, cb_sum_old>) should have produced cb_sum_old tiles. But phase 7 uses default Streaming which pops cb_alpha and reads cb_sum_old in-place. The issue is likely that phase 7 or an earlier phase consumed cb_sum_old incorrectly.

description: Scaler CB design: use two separate scaler CBs (cb_scaler_max=6, cb_scaler_sum=7) each with 1 page, pushed once by reader. Reduce helper never pops scaler, so tiles persist for entire kernel. Avoids the single-CB two-scaler problem where SUM reduce would read the MAX scaler.

Still hanging at Phase 11 add<30,29,31> even with non-in-place output. The BinaryFpu waits on CBs 30 (cb_sum_old) and 29 (cb_sum_new). Possible causes: (1) cb_sum_new has no tiles — the SUM reduce at Phase 10 failed to push output tiles. The SUM reduce uses the matmul path (SUM REDUCE_ROW), and its matmul init might conflict with prior eltwise/matmul state. (2) cb_sum_old has no tiles — Phase 7 mul<cb_sum_old, cb_alpha, cb_sum_old> in-place might have deadlocked. Need DEVICE_PRINT to trace which CB is empty. Linter already added DEVICE_PRINT_PACK markers — run with DEVICE_PRINT enabled.

Full compute kernel rewrite plan: (1) Use 1x1 subblocks with SubblockMajor for QK^T and PV matmuls for correct row-major tile ordering, (2) Use two separate scaler CBs (cb_scaler_max=6, cb_scaler_sum=7), (3) Compute kernel initializes m_old/l_old per Q-block via FillScalar, (4) O accumulation via cb_o_accum + add, (5) Output normalization (O/l_i) per Q-block, (6) Outer loop over work units from RT args, (7) TileRowMajor for 2x2 subblocks to get row-major output
Static analysis of SDPA kernels
finding: Writer total_tiles missing num_q_blocks factor, but test uses num_q_blocks=1. Current CB lifecycle correct for single Q-block.

description: Debug Phase 11 hang
findings: Two issues found: (1) CT arg mismatch between descriptor and compute kernel (descriptor sends [has_mask, B_q_t, B_kv_t, D_t, S_q_tiles, S_kv_tiles] but compute reads [has_mask, B_q_t, B_kv_t, D_t, S_q_tiles, S_kv_tiles] — actually they match). (2) Phase 5 eltwise_chain with OperandKind::Block + Bulk/HeldBulk doesnt produce output in cb_alpha — cascading to Phase 11 hang. Need to fix Phase 5 to use Scalar OperandKind.

error: Compile error: cb_scaler_reduce undefined on line 126 - linter reverted compute kernel to old version with cb_scaler_reduce

description: Linter overwrote my compute kernel with old version. Need to use Edit tool to fix specific lines: cb_scaler_reduce → cb_scaler_sum, remove manual cb_pop_front of scaler (reduce never pops scaler), and fix other issues from the old version.

error: 3 compile errors: (1) CT arg index out of range in reader, (2) scaler CB format not Float16_b, (3) FillScalar incomplete type - missing eltwise_fill.hpp include
Hang - device timeout. Kernels compile but hang at runtime. Need triage report.

description: Hang cause: B_q_t=4 fixed but (1,1,32,32) has only 1 Q-tile. Reader reads out of bounds. Need to clamp block sizes to min(MAX_B, actual_tiles) and pass as RT args. Also need to handle the case where num_q_blocks=1 and B_q_t=1 for single-tile shapes.

error: Compute stuck at matmul_block line 74 (QK^T matmul) waiting for K tiles. Writer stuck at cb_wait_front(cb_out). Shape (1,1,32,32): B_q_t=1, B_kv_t=1, D_t=1. Reader pushes Q, then scalers, then K/V. Matmul with WaitAndRetainOnLastBlock on Q, WaitAndPopPerKBlock on K. Reader should push K after Q+scalers. Possible issue: reader stuck at scaler push (cb_scaler_reduce full?) or K push (cb_k full?). Need DEVICE_PRINT investigation.

description: Analyze SDPA kernel hang
findings: F1: wrong mask accessor CT args (harmless for no-mask test); F2: cb_max_old/cb_sum_old not popped at Q-block end (multi-Q-block issue); F3: writer total_tiles mismatch for non-divisible S_q; F4: o_accum aliased as out+interm. No definitive structural hang found for test case (1,1,32,32).

error: Hang at compute_kernel.cpp:139 (add<cb_o, cb_o_accum, cb_o>). TRISC0 in wait_front, TRISC2 in packer sync (is_pack_reads_per_xy_plane). PV matmul seems to have completed but packer state is wrong. The matmul uses SubblockMajor tile_order with 1x1 subblocks. Pack target is cb_o_accum. Pack issue may be related to mm_block_init at boot (QK^T config) vs PV matmul short init.

details: TRISC0 stuck at matmul_block in0_buf.wait_front (cb_q tiles) at compute.cpp:71. NCRISC stuck at wait_for_brisc_notification (reader not started). BRISC stuck at cb_wait_front on cb_out. The reader (NCRISC) is stuck waiting for BRISC notification — it never started executing the reader kernel.
